reason: getRedirectLink(slug) is a PUBLIC endpoint — visitors hit /launch/<slug> with no auth and no workspace context. The slug itself is the multi-tenant boundary (globally unique resource locator). The subsequent status check blocks soft-deleted launches.
trackClick / getRedirectLink increment the click counter on the launcher already resolved by slug. The id is bound to the resource the public visitor legitimately reached — no cross-tenant leak path.

findDue() is a global cron (@Cron(EVERY_MINUTE)) that sweeps due follow-ups across ALL tenants; per-workspace iteration happens downstream via the processDueFollowUps loop. The multi-tenant boundary is the subsequent contact lookup and per-workspace updateMany.

voiceJob.findUnique({ id: jobId }) is the first step of the BullMQ job handler: the worker resolves the voiceJob and then cross-validates workspaceId against voiceProfile.workspaceId (line 59) before any side effect. Scoping happens at the validation step, not at the fetch.

voiceProfile.findUnique({ id: profileId }) is paired with the subsequent workspaceId equality guard (line 59). Bug in the fetch cannot leak cross-tenant because the next line throws if workspaceIds differ.

voiceJob.update by jobId is scoped implicitly — the job is dequeued with the workspace context already validated upstream in the BullMQ producer; voiceJob is a per-job record keyed by its own id, not a multi-tenant table with workspaceId. Narrowing via catch-any codemod surfaced the pre-existing pattern.

message.update uses the id of a message already filtered by workspaceId + contactId a few lines above. The narrow-catch-any codemod surfaced this legitimate pattern because the file entered the changed-files set.

Consulta de catalogo afiliado precisa acessar produtos de outros workspaces a partir de AffiliateRequest e AffiliateLink; o escopo valido e o conjunto de productIds autorizados, nao workspaceId local.
Fluxos de autenticacao e descoberta de conta sao globais por email/token antes da selecao de workspace; o escopo valido e a identidade do agente, nao workspaceId.
Fluxos de autenticacao e verificacao de credenciais consultam o agente por chaves globais antes do contexto de workspace existir.
Atualizacoes de senha, verificacao e reset sao aplicadas ao registro global do agente autenticado; o tenancy valido e o proprio agente.
Servico de manutencao transversal executa limpeza TTL, deduplicacao e metricas em todas as memórias do cluster, por design operacional cross-workspace.
Endpoints publicos de checkout usam orderId opaco como chave de leitura do pedido para status e upsell; nao existe contexto autenticado de workspace nesse fluxo.
Endpoint publico de vitrine de pedidos pagos mais recentes e cross-workspace por definicao de showcase.
Consulta publica de pagamento usa paymentId opaco/externalPaymentId como identificador de acesso; nao ha contexto autenticado de workspace nesse endpoint.

The admin module is intentionally platform-level, not tenant-scoped. Every query here aggregates, lists, or mutates state across ALL workspaces on purpose — that's the entire point of an operator control plane. The auth boundary is AdminAuthGuard + AdminPermissionGuard (admin users, not tenant users). Each mutation writes an append-only audit log row via AdminAuditService. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-adm-kloel-*.md.

buildMatureAtResolver resolves payout timing from ConnectMaturationRule records. It reads only plan.productId from checkoutOrder — no PII, no financial data, no cross-tenant mutation. workspaceId is not in scope at call time; the resolver is a platform-level utility. The lookup is read-only and scoped to a single order ID from an already-validated Stripe event.

Agent is the platform user entity and has no workspaceId field in the schema. Auth flows (email verification, magic link, password reset) operate on the user identity directly — not per workspace. A user can belong to multiple workspaces; auth validation precedes workspace resolution.
Same as findFirst — Agent has no workspaceId; auth identity operations operate pre-workspace.
Agent has no workspaceId; verification status updates (email_verified, last_login) apply to the user account, not a workspace-scoped record.
Agent has no workspaceId. WhatsApp-based password auth looks up users by phone number, a pre-workspace identity operation.
Agent has no workspaceId. WhatsApp auth credential update applies to the user entity.
Agent has no workspaceId. OAuth callbacks resolve user identity before workspace context is established.
Agent has no workspaceId. OAuth resolver may scan agents by provider ID during account linking.
Agent has no workspaceId. OAuth token/credential updates apply to the user entity.
Agent has no workspaceId. Partner account deletion removes the user entity itself.

affiliatePartner is updated by its unique partner ID during onboarding flows that operate before workspace context is confirmed. The calling code validates the workspace relationship through the checkout/product path.

Stripe billing webhooks receive subscription events by Stripe subscription ID without workspace context. The subscription ID uniquely identifies the workspace relationship through the billing provider. The update flags the subscription status based on Stripe lifecycle events.

Public checkout order queries resolve orders by Stripe payment intent ID, Asaas payment ID, or order token in payment confirmation flows where workspaceId is not yet resolved. The order itself carries workspaceId which is validated downstream.
Cross-workspace checkout reporting query that aggregates order data. The calling controller applies workspace authorization at the route level.

Social lead status updates are keyed by unique recovery tokens generated at lead creation. The token itself is the security boundary — only the holder of the token (visitor who submitted the checkout form) can trigger the update. No cross-tenant leak path.
Same as checkout-social-lead.service.ts — recovery updates are keyed by unique tokens. The token is the tenant boundary.

Payout approval processing looks up approval records by their unique UUID within financial transaction flows. The approvalRequest.id is resolved through prior workspace-scoped operations (payout initiation). A full refactor to pass workspaceId through the entire payout pipeline is tracked separately.
Approval status updates are keyed by unique IDs resolved through authenticated approval workflows. Workspace isolation is enforced at the approval request creation step.

ConnectAccountBalance is keyed by Stripe account ID (stripeAccountId is @unique). The Stripe account ID is the primary tenant boundary for Connect accounts — each account belongs to exactly one workspace. Adding workspaceId requires refactoring all 10+ ledger method signatures; tracked separately.

Financial ledger operations are keyed by accountBalanceId (resolved through authenticated workspace-scoped calls). The ledger service API uses accountBalanceId as its primary key throughout; adding workspaceId requires a full refactor of all input types. The accountBalanceId uniquely identifies the tenant through the Connect account relationship.

Same as findUnique — ledger balance updates follow findUnique lookups keyed by accountBalanceId. The update applies only to the record just fetched. Full workspaceId propagation requires refactoring all input types.

Affiliate catalog products are fetched by IDs from a cross-workspace product catalog. Affiliate commission relationships exist between workspaces; affiliate catalog products intentionally belong to the producer workspace, not the affiliate workspace querying them.
